hidden_gems.name: Goyo Garcia Viadero
hidden_gems.address: Ribera del Duero, Valladolid province, Spain
hidden_gems.description: From one of Ribera del Duero's established winemaking families, Goyo Garcia Viadero set out in 2003 to make natural wine on 40 organically farmed hectares of old Tinto Fino vines. Inspired by Pierre Overnoy of the Jura, he makes wines with wild yeast, no fining, no filtration, and zero added sulfites. His Clarete and Joven de Vinas Viejas have cult followings among natural-wine buyers internationally, while remaining little-known to mainstream Ribera visitors.
hidden_gems.tip: Wines sell out through importers; buy via Cosecha Imports (US) or Vine Trail (UK). Not a public-facing tasting room.

hidden_gems.name: Dominio de Es
hidden_gems.description: Bertrand Sourdais, who also makes Antidoto, launched Dominio de Es sourcing fruit from small pre-phylloxera parcelas around Atauta in Soria. The Vinas Viejas de Soria blends co-fermented Tinto Fino with old-vine Albillo Mayor, aged in Burgundy cooperage. Production is tiny and allocation-only. Decanter reviewed the 2017 vintage positively. One of the most distinctive expressions of the Soria sub-zone available outside Spain.
hidden_gems.tip: Available through specialist Spanish importers; check Voyageurs du Vin (France) and Decanter merchant links for international availability.

hidden_gems.name: Cillar de Silos
hidden_gems.address: Paraje el Soto s/n, 09370 Quintana del Pidio, Burgos, Spain
hidden_gems.description: A family-run estate in Quintana del Pidio, Cillar de Silos makes Ribera del Duero with an emphasis on purity, low yields, and restrained oak. Torresilo is the prestige cuvee; the Crianza is an accessible entry point. Receives consistently strong Decanter reviews but remains under the radar for international wine tourists.
hidden_gems.tip: TripAdvisor reviewers note that stopping by to buy direct is rewarding; call ahead as tour availability varies.

verified.address_quoted: Paraje el Soto s/n, 09370 Quintana del Pidio, Burgos

hidden_gems.name: Bodegas La Horra (Corimbo)
hidden_gems.address: La Horca s/n, 09311 La Horra, Burgos, Spain
hidden_gems.description: La Horra is the Ribera del Duero project of Rioja house Roda, making two wines - Corimbo and Corimbo I - from organically farmed old bush-vine Tinto Fino plots in La Horra, Roa, Anguix, La Aguilera, and Quintana del Pidio. The wines are elegant and mineral rather than opulent, more Burgundian in feel than the thick-extracted style that dominated early-2000s Ribera. Locally celebrated; less promoted internationally than its Rioja sibling.
hidden_gems.tip: Book visits through Roda's tourism office; wines available through Berry Bros & Rudd and Spanish fine wine merchants.

hidden_gems.name: Dominio de Atauta
hidden_gems.address: Ctra. Morcuera s/n, 42345 Atauta, Soria, Spain
hidden_gems.description: Founded in 1999 by Miguel Sanchez after he discovered the abandoned Atauta valley carpeted in ungrafted pre-phylloxera Tinto Fino, Dominio de Atauta manages 43 hectares of vines, approximately 90% of which pre-date the phylloxera crisis (planted 120-180 years ago). Sandy soils at 1,000 metres protected these vines from the louse. Single-vineyard bottlings from named parcelas command collector attention. Soria's highest-profile producer and the flagship of the sub-zone's old-vine revival.
hidden_gems.tip: The Ruta del Vino lists Dominio de Atauta as a recommended winery; check rutadelvinoriberadelduero.es for visit scheduling.

hidden_gems.name: Finca Villacreces
hidden_gems.address: Carretera Soria km 322, 47350 Quintanilla de Onesimo, Valladolid, Spain
hidden_gems.description: Set in the middle of a 200-year-old pine forest at the heart of Ribera del Duero's Golden Mile, Finca Villacreces is a striking estate little known outside Spain. The historic finca includes 75 hectares of Tinto Fino, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot. Wine tourism experiences here centre on the forest setting as much as the wine cellar. Pruno is the accessible entry label; the Gran Reserva bottling draws critical attention.
hidden_gems.tip: The pine forest makes this a particularly atmospheric visit in autumn (October-November) when colours peak at harvest time.

verified.address_quoted: Carretera Soria km 322, Quintanilla de Onesimo, Valladolid 47350

hidden_gems.name: Dominio del Aguila
hidden_gems.address: Calle Los Lagares 42, 09370 La Aguilera, Burgos, Spain
hidden_gems.description: Founded in 2010 by Jorge Monzon (former Vega Sicilia and Arzuaga winemaker trained in Burgundy) and architect Isabel Rodero, Dominio del Aguila farms 66 certified-organic hectares of old-vine Tinto Fino in La Aguilera. Three restored 15th-century cellars house fermentation and ageing. Their Picaro del Aguila rosado and Reserva have attracted strong critical attention. The estate represents a new generation of precision Ribera del Duero combining Burgundian sensibility with Castilian terroir.
hidden_gems.tip: Contact the estate directly for visits; not on the mass-tourism circuit. US importer is European Cellars (Charlotte, NC).

verified.address_quoted: Calle Los Lagares, 42, La Aguilera, Burgos

hidden_gems.name: Bodegas Valduero
hidden_gems.address: Ctra Aranda s/n, 09443 Gumiel de Mercado, Burgos, Spain
hidden_gems.description: Founded in 1984 by Gregorio Garcia Alvarez, Bodegas Valduero in Gumiel de Mercado is one of Ribera del Duero's quieter estates: its estate Tinto Fino produces wines across the full range from Dos Anos joven to Ultra Premium Gran Reserva. The estate is committed to single-variety Tinto Fino and multi-year barrel ageing. Rarely discussed in international press despite consistent quality; largely sustained by loyal Spanish restaurant accounts.
hidden_gems.tip: Visit the estate cellar in Gumiel de Mercado; they offer guided tours and bottle sales direct. Website: valduero.com.

verified.address_quoted: Ctra Aranda s/n, Gumiel de Mercado, 09443, Burgos
